items.title: Roberto De Zerbi leaves Marseille after PSG hammering and Champions League exit
items.summary: Marseille beaten 5-0 by PSG and 3-0 by Club Brugge De Zerbi joined as head coach in 2024 after Brighton exit Roberto De Zerbi has left Marseille in the wake of a 5-0 thrashing at the hands of Paris Saint-Germain. The nine-time French champions said on Wednesday that they had ended “their collaboration by mutual agreement.” The heavy loss Sunday at the Parc des Princes restored PSG’s two-point lead over Lens after 21 rounds, with Marseille fourth. De Zerbi’s team also endured an embarrassing 3-0 loss at Club Brugge two weeks ago that resulted in Marseille exiting the Champions League. Continue reading...

items.title: 'Gênante cijfers in eigen huis en giftige situatie: Spurs kan zomaar degraderen'
items.summary: In de nieuwe aflevering van VI ZSM blikken Tim Tempelaars en Jarno Verweij terug op de Premier League-wedstrijd Tottenham Hotspur-Newcastle United. In Londen was Newcastle met 2-1 te sterk voor de ploeg van manager Thomas Frank, die daardoor nog meer onder druk komt te staan.

items.title: 'Ook Robin van Persie houdt tegenwoordig van lelijk winnen'
items.summary: Hans Kraay Junior. Van speler, tot amateurtrainer, tot ESPN-analyticus. Altijd op de hoogte van de laatste ontwikkelingen, met immer een uitgesproken mening neemt hij wekelijks in zijn column de actualiteit door met beurtelings VI’s Freek Jansen en Martijn Krabbendam.

items.title: 'Reeled in!' | Big Fish POV with Luke Littler
items.summary: Take a look at what Luke Littler sees when he hits a 170 finish.

items.title: 'Meedogenloze Cruijff drukt direct stevig zijn stempel bij Ajax'
items.summary: In de nieuwe aflevering van VI ZSM bespreken Tim Tempelaars en Jarno Verweij de laatste ontwikkelingen binnen Ajax. De Amsterdammers hebben dinsdagavond een fonkelnieuwe technische staf voor Jong Ajax gepresenteerd. Na het ontslag van trainer Willem Weijs maakt de club het seizoen af met een Spaanse staf, onder leiding van hoofdtrainer Oscar García. En dat is dan weer een zeer goede bekende van Jordi Cruijff.

items.title: Carrick hails 'unbelievable' Sesko goal to earn draw
items.summary: Michael Carrick hailed super-sub Benjamin Sesko's "unbelievable finish" after the striker's amazing last-gasp volley snatched Manchester United a 1-1 draw at West Ham United.

items.title: Frank 'convinced' of Spurs stay amid Poch chants
items.summary: Tottenham head coach Thomas Frank said he is "convinced" he will remain in charge for the north London derby against Arsenal despite Spurs fans calling for him to be sacked.

items.title: De Zerbi leaves Marseille after 5-0 loss to PSG
items.summary: Marseille head coach Roberto De Zerbi has left the club following their 5-0 defeat to Paris Saint Germain on Sunday.

items.title: Brouwers weigert plannen Heerenveen bij te stellen: 'Wat ben ik dan voor gozer?'
items.summary: SC Heerenveen speelt woensdagavond de inhaalwedstrijd bij Go Ahead Eagles en móét winnen in Deventer: om de vervelende nasmaak van de afgang van zaterdag bij FC Twente weg te spoelen, maar ook omdat het de punten simpelweg hard nodig heeft voor het behalen van zijn doelstelling. Die is en die blijft: het halen van Europees voetbal.

items.title: What is the most expensive combined substitution in football history? | The Knowledge
items.summary: Plus: a perfect hat-trick of assists, more almost-one-club players and Oxford’s penalty drought Mail us with your questions and answers “In their Champions League match against PSV Eindhoven, Bayern Munich made four substitutions in the 62nd minute,” writes Stephan Wijnen. “The four players entering the pitch together had a combined estimated value of €265m (Harry Kane, Michael Olise, Serge Gnabry and Alphonso Davies). Is this the most expensive combined substitution ever?” Before we go any further – a player’s estimated value is not an objective measure, but using transfer fees doesn’t necessarily work, with some players moving for no fee (Kylian Mbappé, for example). Like Stephan in his question, we are going to use Transfermarkt’s valuations in a bid for consistency, and will focus on the value of players coming on. Can you do any better? Email us with your answers Continue reading...

items.title: Carrick licht United-uitblinker uit na volgende goal in 'Fergie Time'
items.summary: Interim-manager Michael Carrick behield dinsdagavond tegen West Ham United zijn ongeslagen status met Manchester United dankzij een goal van Benjamin Sesko, maar de Engelsman was na afloop vooral lovend over een verdediger in zijn ploeg.

items.title: De Zerbi leaves Marseille by mutual consent
items.summary: Roberto de Zerbi, who led Marseille to second in Ligue 1 last season, leaves following their Champions League exit and a 5-0 defeat by Paris St-Germain.

items.title: Wemby drops 40 to lead Spurs to win over Lakers
items.summary: Victor Wembanyama scored 37 of his 40 points in the first half as the San Antonio Spurs beat the Los Angeles Lakers.

items.title: West Ham expose the limitations of Michael Carrick's Man United squad
items.summary: A point on the road isn't a bad result, especially after four wins in a row, but Tuesday's draw with West Ham has shown that Manchester United still have issues breaking down a packed defense.

items.title: Has time run out for Frank? Spurs' latest loss puts him in unprecedented danger
items.summary: After Tottenham's 2-1 loss to Newcastle, it's fair to wonder how long they can continue with Thomas Frank.

items.title: Are Hearts developing the habit of champions?
items.summary: Does Heart of Midlothian's late derby winner against Hibernian suggest they are developing the habit of champions as they go six points clear at the top of the Scottish Premiership?

items.title: Carrick's 'Sir Alex' playbook and a happy start at Man Utd
items.summary: Michael Carrick is happy with his start as Manchester United head coach despite dropping his first points in 1-1 draw at West Ham.

items.title: Sesko’s nonchalance late strike show of resilience that enhances Carrick’s cause | Barney Ronay
items.summary: The winning run may be over but his Manchester United team showed fight until the end against West Ham On nights such as these it can feel as though football is choosing to remind you of its true nature. Which is, it turns out, the most gloriously perverse, slow-burn, 400‑miles‑from‑home, 10.15pm on a Tuesday, waving your arms in the air, gripped‑with‑final‑plot‑twist-ecstasy pursuit ever devised. For Manchester United’s travelling support this game must have felt like a slow-motion strangulation. Your team have had two shots on target all night. They’re 1-0 down against relegation-haunted West Ham – 95 minutes have passed. Narratives are being muddled. Arcs of hope reined in. Continue reading...
